scope: AgenC-branded base system prompt content, dynamic prompt section registry, and effective-prompt override precedence.

requiredBehaviors: AgenC emits intro, system, doing-tasks, actions, tool-use, tone, and output-efficiency sections before the dynamic boundary.
The doing-tasks section is suppressed when an output-style prompt is present.
The simple-mode path emits only the intro, boundary, and environment sections.
edgeCases: Visible tool guidance is conditional on the enabled tool names.
Slim shell-only sessions do not receive unavailable file/search tool instructions.

requiredBehaviors: Session guidance, permissions, autonomous mode, memory, project instructions, environment, language, output style, MCP instructions, and scratchpad content resolve after the boundary.
Volatile dynamic sections recompute each call so session-scoped data does not leak across turns.
Memoized sections remain cached until explicit cache clearing.
edgeCases: Connected MCP servers without instructions are ignored.
Cache clearing resets both cached non-null values and cached null values.

requiredBehaviors: Override prompts replace all other prompt content.
Agent prompts take precedence over custom and default prompts.
Custom prompts replace the default prompt when no agent prompt is active.
Append prompts are appended unless an override prompt replaced the prompt entirely.
edgeCases: Empty custom prompts fall back to default sections.
String and string-array prompt inputs normalize to the branded SystemPrompt array.

carriedBehavior: AgenC prompt assembly returns an ordered section list and joined text with a single dynamic boundary.
Static content remains cache-prefix friendly while dynamic content is resolved after the boundary.
Environment, language, output style, MCP, scratchpad, project-instruction, memory, permissions, and autonomous sections are driven by explicit runtime inputs.
The section registry distinguishes cached sections from volatile sections and exposes cache clearing for command/runtime hooks.
Effective prompt composition preserves override, agent, custom, default, and append precedence.
intentionalReductions: Product-specific help, feedback, docs-map, model-launch, internal-user, and experimental feature-gate branches are not carried because AgenC has separate owned surfaces or no live subsystem for them.
Hook, proactive, brief, skill-discovery, and embedded-search prompt branches are not revived as inert placeholders.
Provider-specific model-family recommendations are left to provider adapters and configuration instead of hard-coded system prompt text.
Compact summarizer compatibility keeps a local helper in runtime/src/session/_deps/system-prompt.ts; the primary runtime prompt module owns shared prompt content and precedence behavior.

commands: npm exec --workspace=@tetsuo-ai/runtime vitest run src/prompts/system-prompt.test.ts src/prompts/sections.test.ts
